{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FrqQTEaf1oTQzkEjGpx3pm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"9327bb17-678d-4954-b39e-6cc72cb1c01c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:00:07.287Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018H9UaXStxStAEuarekdAYr","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Testing\n    32→- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n    33→- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n    34→- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n    35→- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n    36→- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n    37→- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n    38→- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n    39→- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n    40→- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n    41→\n    42→## Browser Testing\n    43→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    44→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    45→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    46→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    47→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    48→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    49→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    50→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    51→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    52→\n    53→## Deployment\n    54→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    55→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    56→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    57→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    58→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    59→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    60→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    61→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    62→\n    63→## React + SQLite\n    64→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    65→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    66→\n    67→## Security\n    68→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    69→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    70→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    71→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    72→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    73→\n    74→## Working Directory\n    75→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    76→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    77→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"d5429b71-b824-4b3c-b805-04aaa6055ff6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:00:07.822Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Testing\n- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017H3CzAyvAfJCkCfoNx992k","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Registered Platforms\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub\n     4→- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     5→- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n     6→- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n     7→\n     8→## Production Deployment\n     9→- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n    10→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    11→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n    12→- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n    13→- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n    14→- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n    15→- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n    16→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"f0211442-3457-48c3-813a-ef3382408097","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:00:08.174Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/platforms.md","content":"# Registered Platforms\n\n## GitHub\n- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n\n## Production Deployment\n- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n","numLines":16,"startLine":1,"totalLines":16}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NGhgY7wVoaZvhykvMVVrm9","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-24 00:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 7: found and fixed 4 security/correctness bugs. Created issue #88, implemented fixes in PR #89, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 198 tests pass\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo (different project), Google Workspace promo\n    12→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    13→\n    14→## Work Completed\n    15→\n    16→### Issue #88: Code review round 7 (PR #89 — merged + deployed)\n    17→\n    18→4 bugs fixed:\n    19→\n    20→1. **HTML injection in Telegram messages** (CRITICAL) — Book titles were interpolated directly into HTML-mode Telegram messages (`<b>${bookTitle}</b>`). Added `escapeHtml()` helper to sanitize `<`, `>`, `&`, `\"` characters. Applied to all user content in Telegram webhook route.\n    21→\n    22→2. **Telegram /start auth bypass** (IMPORTANT) — `/start listing_ID` command linked any group to any listing without checking ownership. Now verifies the sender is the listing author via `telegram_user_links` table. Non-authors get a message to use `/link` instead.\n    23→\n    24→3. **Discord guildId/channelId path traversal** (IMPORTANT) — `guildId` and `channelId` from webhook payload were used unvalidated in Discord API URL paths (`/guilds/${guildId}/channels`). Added `isValidSnowflake()` validation (17-20 digit numeric strings).\n    25→\n    26→4. **Missing discord_link in profile reading** (IMPORTANT) — Profile reading API returned `telegram_link` but not `discord_link`. Added to SQL query and TypeScript interface.\n    27→\n    28→All 198 tests pass (1 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n    29→\n    30→## Issues Monitored\n    31→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n    32→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    33→\n    34→## Next Session Priority\n    35→1. Check production health\n    36→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    37→3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n    38→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"fde3c0ba-300d-4845-841a-cf5630fae34a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:00:14.327Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-0000.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 00:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 7: found and fixed 4 security/correctness bugs. Created issue #88, implemented fixes in PR #89, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 198 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo (different project), Google Workspace promo\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #88: Code review round 7 (PR #89 — merged + deployed)\n\n4 bugs fixed:\n\n1. **HTML injection in Telegram messages** (CRITICAL) — Book titles were interpolated directly into HTML-mode Telegram messages (`<b>${bookTitle}</b>`). Added `escapeHtml()` helper to sanitize `<`, `>`, `&`, `\"` characters. Applied to all user content in Telegram webhook route.\n\n2. **Telegram /start auth bypass** (IMPORTANT) — `/start listing_ID` command linked any group to any listing without checking ownership. Now verifies the sender is the listing author via `telegram_user_links` table. Non-authors get a message to use `/link` instead.\n\n3. **Discord guildId/channelId path traversal** (IMPORTANT) — `guildId` and `channelId` from webhook payload were used unvalidated in Discord API URL paths (`/guilds/${guildId}/channels`). Added `isValidSnowflake()` validation (17-20 digit numeric strings).\n\n4. **Missing discord_link in profile reading** (IMPORTANT) — Profile reading API returned `telegram_link` but not `discord_link`. Added to SQL query and TypeScript interface.\n\nAll 198 tests pass (1 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":38,"startLine":1,"totalLines":38}}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"message_stop"},"session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"b73f1277-6dd1-4f3a-8581-f8471df97ab2"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CJQGn1hVWD23beNKkGQ1zN","type":"tool_result","content":"title:\tAction Required: GitHub PAT expires in 7 days\nstate:\tOPEN\nauthor:\tTommyBrennan\nlabels:\tneeds-human\ncomments:\t2\nassignees:\t\nprojects:\tBookmate (Todo)\nmilestone:\t\nnumber:\t54\n--\n## What's needed\n\nThe GitHub personal access token (classic) **\"test-instance\"** used by the agent will **expire in ~7 days** (around March 27, 2026).\n\n## Why\n\nWithout a valid PAT, the agent cannot:\n- Push commits to the repository\n- Create/merge pull requests\n- Manage issues and project board\n- Run any `gh` CLI commands\n\n## How to fix\n\n1. Visit https://github.com/settings/tokens/3809420952/regenerate\n2. Generate a new token with the same scopes (delete:packages, notifications, project, repo, workflow, write:discussion, write:packages)\n3. Update the token in `.claude-beat/.env` on the server:\n   ```\n   GH_TOKEN=<new-token>\n   ```\n4. Update the git remote URL:\n   ```\n   git remote set-url origin https://<new-token>@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git\n   ```\n\n## Priority\n\n**High** — If not renewed before expiry, all agent operations will stop.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"2efc10f7-d191-43b9-9b78-81de50b5d216","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:00:27.096Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"title:\tAction Required: GitHub PAT expires in 7 days\nstate:\tOPEN\nauthor:\tTommyBrennan\nlabels:\tneeds-human\ncomments:\t2\nassignees:\t\nprojects:\tBookmate (Todo)\nmilestone:\t\nnumber:\t54\n--\n## What's needed\n\nThe GitHub personal access token (classic) **\"test-instance\"** used by the agent will **expire in ~7 days** (around March 27, 2026).\n\n## Why\n\nWithout a valid PAT, the agent cannot:\n- Push commits to the repository\n- Create/merge pull requests\n- Manage issues and project board\n- Run any `gh` CLI commands\n\n## How to fix\n\n1. Visit https://github.com/settings/tokens/3809420952/regenerate\n2. Generate a new token with the same scopes (delete:packages, notifications, project, repo, workflow, write:discussion, write:packages)\n3. Update the token in `.claude-beat/.env` on the server:\n   ```\n   GH_TOKEN=<new-token>\n   ```\n4. Update the git remote URL:\n   ```\n   git remote set-url origin https://<new-token>@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git\n   ```\n\n## Priority\n\n**High** — If not renewed before expiry, all agent operations will stop.","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

Business logic - group formation, notifications, listing lifecycle edge cases\n6. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XfFGC7xLGwYmRwzBdhHc9F","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    87→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    88→  );\n    89→`);\n    90→\n    91→// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\n    92→try {\n    93→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n    94→} catch {\n    95→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\n    99→try {\n   100→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n   101→} catch {\n   102→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\n   106→try {\n   107→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   108→} catch {\n   109→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→// Idempotent migration: add discord_channel_id column\n   113→try {\n   114→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   115→} catch {\n   116→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→// Idempotent migration: add telegram_chat_id column for auto-telegram feature\n   120→try {\n   121→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n   122→} catch {\n   123→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   131→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   132→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n   133→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n   134→  );\n   135→\n   136→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n   137→    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n   138→    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   139→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n   140→  );\n   141→\n   142→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_user_links (\n   143→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   144→    telegram_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   145→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   146→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id),\n   147→    UNIQUE (telegram_user_id),\n   148→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   149→  );\n   150→`);\n   151→\n   152→// Rating and Reputation system\n   153→db.exec(`\n   154→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n   155→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n   156→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   157→    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   158→    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   159→    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n   160→    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   161→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   162→    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n   163→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n   164→    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n   165→    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   166→  );\n   167→`);\n   168→\n   169→// Performance indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n   170→db.exec(`\n   171→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\n   172→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\n   173→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\n   174→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n   175→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_applications_listing_status ON listing_applications(listing_id, status);\n   176→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread ON notifications(user_id, is_read);\n   177→`);\n   178→\n   179→export default db;\n   180→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"b66d92de-d887-449c-bba6-377a54373edd","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:01:08.562Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0143KxRX5G1MF9ngkQMBiAR7","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→\n     4→export interface SessionData {\n     5→  userId?: number;\n     6→  email?: string;\n     7→  displayName?: string;\n     8→}\n     9→\n    10→const DEV_SECRET =\n    11→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    12→\n    13→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    14→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    15→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    16→    throw new Error(\n    17→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→  return {\n    21→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    22→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    23→    cookieOptions: {\n    24→      httpOnly: true,\n    25→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n    26→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    27→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    28→    },\n    29→  };\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→export async function getSession() {\n    33→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    34→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    35→}\n    36→\n    37→export async function requireAuth() {\n    38→  const session = await getSession();\n    39→  if (!session.userId) {\n    40→    return null;\n    41→  }\n    42→  return session;\n    43→}\n    44→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"71e0f00c-7173-4650-9a67-7d7931092c9d","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:01:08.922Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Ng4uS9aeeQp2tpKf5xfTtR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→\n     4→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     5→  const user = db\n     6→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     7→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     8→  return user?.email || null;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→export function createNotification(\n    12→  userId: number,\n    13→  listingId: number | null,\n    14→  type: string,\n    15→  message: string\n    16→) {\n    17→  db.prepare(\n    18→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    19→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    20→\n    21→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    22→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    23→  if (email) {\n    24→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    25→    sendEmail(email, subject, message).catch(() => {\n    26→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    27→    });\n    28→  }\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    32→  switch (type) {\n    33→    case \"new_member\":\n    34→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    35→    case \"group_full\":\n    36→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    37→    case \"application_received\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    39→    case \"application_approved\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    41→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    43→    case \"rating_received\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    45→    case \"listing_deleted\":\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been cancelled\";\n    47→    case \"listing_updated\":\n    48→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been updated\";\n    49→    case \"telegram_ready\":\n    50→      return \"Bookmate: Your Telegram reading group is ready!\";\n    51→    case \"discord_ready\":\n    52→      return \"Bookmate: Your Discord channel is ready!\";\n    53→    default:\n    54→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    55→  }\n    56→}\n    57→\n    58→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    59→  listingId: number,\n    60→  joinerName: string\n    61→) {\n    62→  const listing = db\n    63→    .prepare(\n    64→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    65→    )\n    66→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    67→\n    68→  if (listing) {\n    69→    createNotification(\n    70→      listing.author_id,\n    71→      listingId,\n    72→      \"new_member\",\n    73→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    74→    );\n    75→  }\n    76→}\n    77→\n    78→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    79→  listingId: number,\n    80→  applicantName: string\n    81→) {\n    82→  const listing = db\n    83→    .prepare(\n    84→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    85→    )\n    86→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    87→\n    88→  if (listing) {\n    89→    createNotification(\n    90→      listing.author_id,\n    91→      listingId,\n    92→      \"application_received\",\n    93→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    94→    );\n    95→  }\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n    99→  userId: number,\n   100→  listingId: number,\n   101→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n   102→) {\n   103→  const listing = db\n   104→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   105→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   106→\n   107→  if (listing) {\n   108→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   109→    const message =\n   110→      decision === \"approved\"\n   111→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   112→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   113→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   114→  }\n   115→}\n   116→\n   117→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   118→  const listing = db\n   119→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   120→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string; author_id: number } | undefined;\n   121→  // Exclude the author — they already received a separate \"new_member\" notification\n   122→  const members = db\n   123→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != ?\")\n   124→    .all(listingId, listing?.author_id ?? -1) as { user_id: number }[];\n   125→\n   126→  if (listing) {\n   127→    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n   128→    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n   129→      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n   130→      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   131→    const message = botConfigured\n   132→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n   133→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n   134→\n   135→    for (const member of members) {\n   136→      createNotification(\n   137→        member.user_id,\n   138→        listingId,\n   139→        \"group_full\",\n   140→        message\n   141→      );\n   142→    }\n   143→  }\n   144→}\n   145→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"f18baeb2-4411-4463-912c-700d4f3926d0","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:01:09.060Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0121ceiypVge4nJjd6dfVocn","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01CpsJ4xhRdfgbBpX7qm6btR","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4771,"cache_read_input_tokens":11613,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4771,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":39,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"7a13f32a-eaf0-4c37-b5e7-0929a9c18d25"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CpsJ4xhRdfgbBpX7qm6btR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n    30→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    42→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Check if already a member\n    46→  const existing = db\n    47→    .prepare(\n    48→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    )\n    50→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    51→\n    52→  if (existing) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Check if user has a pending application (e.g., requires_approval was toggled off)\n    57→  const pendingApp = db\n    58→    .prepare(\n    59→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    60→    )\n    61→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    62→\n    63→  if (pendingApp) {\n    64→    return NextResponse.json(\n    65→      { error: \"You already have a pending application for this group\" },\n    66→      { status: 400 }\n    67→    );\n    68→  }\n    69→\n    70→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    71→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    72→    const { count } = db\n    73→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    74→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    75→\n    76→    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    77→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    78→    }\n    79→\n    80→    db.prepare(\n    81→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    82→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    83→\n    84→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    85→    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    86→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n    90→  });\n    91→\n    92→  const result = joinTransaction();\n    93→\n    94→  if (result.error) {\n    95→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n    96→  }\n    97→\n    98→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n    99→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   100→\n   101→  if (result.count === (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n   102→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n   103→  }\n   104→\n   105→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n   106→}\n   107→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"fe30ce37-e38b-42bc-89e1-dfc20521629c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:01:11.840Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0121ceiypVge4nJjd6dfVocn","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0177M5ibDFAUo1Hqu9vnRd2N","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4771,"cache_read_input_tokens":11613,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4771,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":39,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"81728d0f-901e-4801-b6d6-7d6b5aa4d233"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0177M5ibDFAUo1Hqu9vnRd2N","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationReceived } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json(\n    31→      { error: \"You cannot apply to your own listing\" },\n    32→      { status: 400 }\n    33→    );\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (!listing.requires_approval) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"This group does not require approval. Join directly.\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    44→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    45→  }\n    46→\n    47→  // Wrap membership check + application in a transaction to prevent TOCTOU races\n    48→  const applyTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    49→    // Check if already a member\n    50→    const isMember = db\n    51→      .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    52→      .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    53→\n    54→    if (isMember) {\n    55→      return { error: \"You are already a member\", status: 400 };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    // Re-check is_full inside transaction\n    59→    const currentListing = db\n    60→      .prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    61→      .get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n    62→\n    63→    if (currentListing?.is_full) {\n    64→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", status: 400 };\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    // Check if already applied (pending)\n    68→    const existingApp = db\n    69→      .prepare(\n    70→        \"SELECT id, status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    71→      )\n    72→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { id: number; status: string } | undefined;\n    73→\n    74→    if (existingApp) {\n    75→      if (existingApp.status === \"pending\") {\n    76→        return { error: \"You have already applied\", status: 400 };\n    77→      }\n    78→      if (existingApp.status === \"approved\") {\n    79→        return { error: \"You are already approved\", status: 400 };\n    80→      }\n    81→      // If rejected, allow re-application by updating status back to pending\n    82→      db.prepare(\n    83→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'pending', decided_at = NULL, applied_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    84→      ).run(existingApp.id);\n    85→\n    86→      return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    // Insert new application\n    90→    db.prepare(\n    91→      \"INSERT INTO listing_applications (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    92→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    93→\n    94→    return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    95→  });\n    96→\n    97→  const result = applyTransaction();\n    98→\n    99→  if (result.error) {\n   100→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: result.status });\n   101→  }\n   102→\n   103→  // Notify outside transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   104→  notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   105→\n   106→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   107→}\n   108→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"cdf180c4-95ec-45c7-a7b3-affa33d88787","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:01:12.356Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"aa8122251812e89bc","tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","description":"Reading app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":16634,"tool_uses":8,"duration_ms":10047},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"6b5aec11-483b-4047-a5a4-cd55bb08b8a9","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0121ceiypVge4nJjd6dfVocn","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018MhN19YL1PgaZGAc5HaRNR","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4771,"cache_read_input_tokens":11613,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4771,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":39,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"863cf27f-4172-4772-ad03-3614f55c7ffe"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018MhN19YL1PgaZGAc5HaRNR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function PATCH(\n     7→  req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const body = await req.json();\n    34→  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n    35→\n    36→  if (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n    44→  const application = db\n    45→    .prepare(\n    46→      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    47→    )\n    48→    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    49→\n    50→  if (!application) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n    53→      { status: 404 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n    58→\n    59→  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    60→    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    61→    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    62→      // Check if group is already full\n    63→      const { count } = db\n    64→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    65→        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    66→\n    67→      if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    68→        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      // Update application status\n    72→      db.prepare(\n    73→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    74→      ).run(applicationId);\n    75→\n    76→      // Add to members\n    77→      db.prepare(\n    78→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    79→      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n    80→\n    81→      // Check if group is now full\n    82→      const newCount = count + 1;\n    83→      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n    84→      if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    85→        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    86→\n    87→        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n    88→        rejectedApps = db\n    89→          .prepare(\n    90→            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    91→          )\n    92→          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n    93→\n    94→        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n    95→          db.prepare(\n    96→            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    97→          ).run(app.id);\n    98→        }\n    99→\n   100→        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n   101→      }\n   102→      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n   103→    });\n   104→\n   105→    try {\n   106→      const result = approveTransaction();\n   107→\n   108→      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n   109→      try {\n   110→        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n   111→\n   112→        if (result.isFull) {\n   113→          notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n   114→          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n   115→            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   116→          }\n   117→        }\n   118→      } catch (notifErr) {\n   119→        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n   120→      }\n   121→\n   122→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   123→    } catch (err) {\n   124→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n   125→      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n   126→    }\n   127→  } else {\n   128→    // Reject\n   129→    db.prepare(\n   130→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   131→    ).run(applicationId);\n   132→\n   133→    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   134→\n   135→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   136→  }\n   137→}\n   138→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"e9f95151-856f-473c-808a-7a8503a4bb22","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:01:12.521Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"aa8122251812e89bc","tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","description":"Reading app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":21413,"tool_uses":9,"duration_ms":12874},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"05943564-1bf5-4434-80a6-8021dd103b46","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01BvXcsHbpDhaVYBmSQhgFBG","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01XXfjK9J8Wn6uqAEJqgHbvg","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4743,"cache_read_input_tokens":16384,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4743,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"38b24e3a-b964-4d9f-bb99-dbbb7b999cfb"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XXfjK9J8Wn6uqAEJqgHbvg","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession, requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→import { isBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     6→import { isBotConfigured as isDiscordBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     7→\n     8→export async function GET(\n     9→  _req: NextRequest,\n    10→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    11→) {\n    12→  const { id } = await params;\n    13→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    14→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\n    20→      `SELECT\n    21→        l.*,\n    22→        u.display_name as author_name\n    23→      FROM listings l\n    24→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    25→      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    26→    )\n    27→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    28→\n    29→  if (!listing) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const members = db\n    34→    .prepare(\n    35→      `SELECT u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, lm.joined_at\n    36→       FROM listing_members lm\n    37→       JOIN users u ON lm.user_id = u.id\n    38→       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n    39→       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    40→    )\n    41→    .all(listingId) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n    42→\n    43→  const session = await getSession();\n    44→  const isMember = session.userId\n    45→    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    46→    : false;\n    47→  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    48→    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    49→    : false;\n    50→\n    51→  // PRD: full listings should be invisible to non-participants\n    52→  if (listing.is_full && !isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json(\n    54→      { error: \"This listing is no longer available\" },\n    55→      { status: 404 }\n    56→    );\n    57→  }\n    58→\n    59→  // Check if the current user has an application (regardless of requires_approval,\n    60→  // since the flag may have been toggled after the application was submitted)\n    61→  let hasApplied = false;\n    62→  let applicationStatus = \"\";\n    63→  if (session.userId && !isMember) {\n    64→    const app = db\n    65→      .prepare(\n    66→        \"SELECT status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    67→      )\n    68→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { status: string } | undefined;\n    69→    if (app) {\n    70→      hasApplied = app.status === \"pending\";\n    71→      applicationStatus = app.status;\n    72→    }\n    73→  }\n    74→\n    75→  // Fetch pending applicants if the viewer is the author\n    76→  let pendingApplicants: { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[] = [];\n    77→  if (isAuthor && listing.requires_approval) {\n    78→    pendingApplicants = db\n    79→      .prepare(\n    80→        `SELECT la.id as application_id, u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, la.applied_at\n    81→         FROM listing_applications la\n    82→         JOIN users u ON la.user_id = u.id\n    83→         WHERE la.listing_id = ? AND la.status = 'pending'\n    84→         ORDER BY la.applied_at ASC`\n    85→      )\n    86→      .all(listingId) as { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[];\n    87→  }\n    88→\n    89→  // Strip platform links for non-members to prevent leaking invite URLs\n    90→  const safeListingData = { ...listing };\n    91→  if (!isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    92→    delete safeListingData.telegram_link;\n    93→    delete safeListingData.discord_link;\n    94→  }\n    95→\n    96→  return NextResponse.json({\n    97→    listing: {\n    98→      ...safeListingData,\n    99→      members,\n   100→      memberCount: members.length,\n   101→      isMember,\n   102→      isAuthor,\n   103→      hasApplied,\n   104→      applicationStatus,\n   105→      pendingApplicants,\n   106→      currentUserId: session.userId || null,\n   107→      telegramBotConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n   108→      discordBotConfigured: isDiscordBotConfigured(),\n   109→    },\n   110→  });\n   111→}\n   112→\n   113→export async function PATCH(\n   114→  req: NextRequest,\n   115→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   116→) {\n   117→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   118→  if (!session) {\n   119→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   120→  }\n   121→\n   122→  const { id } = await params;\n   123→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   124→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   125→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   126→  }\n   127→\n   128→  const listing = db\n   129→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   130→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   131→\n   132→  if (!listing) {\n   133→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n   134→  }\n   135→\n   136→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n   137→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can edit this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n   138→  }\n   139→\n   140→  // Cannot edit if a platform link has been shared (group already formed)\n   141→  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n   142→    return NextResponse.json(\n   143→      { error: \"Cannot edit a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   144→      { status: 400 }\n   145→    );\n   146→  }\n   147→\n   148→  try {\n   149→    const body = await req.json();\n   150→    const {\n   151→      language,\n   152→      readingPace,\n   153→      startDate,\n   154→      meetingFormat,\n   155→      maxGroupSize,\n   156→      requiresApproval,\n   157→      platformPreference,\n   158→    } = body;\n   159→\n   160→    // Validate fields that are provided\n   161→    if (readingPace !== undefined && (!readingPace || readingPace.length > 200)) {\n   162→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Reading pace is required (max 200 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   163→    }\n   164→\n   165→    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n   166→      if (!/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n   167→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   168→      }\n   169→      const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n   170→      if (startDate < today) {\n   171→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Start date cannot be in the past\" }, { status: 400 });\n   172→      }\n   173→    }\n   174→\n   175→    if (meetingFormat !== undefined && ![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n   176→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid meeting format\" }, { status: 400 });\n   177→    }\n   178→\n   179→    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n   180→      const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n   181→      if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n   182→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" }, { status: 400 });\n   183→      }\n   184→\n   185→      // Cannot reduce below current member count\n   186→      const memberCount = (db\n   187→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   188→        .get(listingId) as { count: number }).count;\n   189→\n   190→      if (size < memberCount) {\n   191→        return NextResponse.json(\n   192→          { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${memberCount})` },\n   193→          { status: 400 }\n   194→        );\n   195→      }\n   196→    }\n   197→\n   198→    if (language !== undefined && (!language || typeof language !== \"string\" || language.length > 100)) {\n   199→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Language is required (max 100 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   200→    }\n   201→\n   202→    if (platformPreference !== undefined && ![\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)) {\n   203→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid platform preference\" }, { status: 400 });\n   204→    }\n   205→\n   206→    // Build dynamic UPDATE\n   207→    const updates: string[] = [];\n   208→    const values: unknown[] = [];\n   209→\n   210→    if (language !== undefined) {\n   211→      updates.push(\"language = ?\");\n   212→      values.push(language);\n   213→    }\n   214→    if (readingPace !== undefined) {\n   215→      updates.push(\"reading_pace = ?\");\n   216→      values.push(readingPace.trim());\n   217→    }\n   218→    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n   219→      updates.push(\"start_date = ?\");\n   220→      values.push(startDate);\n   221→    }\n   222→    if (meetingFormat !== undefined) {\n   223→      updates.push(\"meeting_format = ?\");\n   224→      values.push(meetingFormat);\n   225→    }\n   226→    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n   227→      const newSize = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n   228→      updates.push(\"max_group_size = ?\");\n   229→      values.push(newSize);\n   230→\n   231→      // If increasing group size on a full listing, reopen it\n   232→      const currentMemberCount = (db\n   233→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   234→        .get(listingId) as { count: number }).count;\n   235→      if (newSize > currentMemberCount && listing.is_full) {\n   236→        updates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n   237→        values.push(0);\n   238→      }\n   239→    }\n   240→    if (requiresApproval !== undefined) {\n   241→      updates.push(\"requires_approval = ?\");\n   242→      values.push(requiresApproval ? 1 : 0);\n   243→    }\n   244→    if (platformPreference !== undefined) {\n   245→      updates.push(\"platform_preference = ?\");\n   246→      values.push(platformPreference);\n   247→    }\n   248→\n   249→    if (updates.length === 0) {\n   250→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"No fields to update\" }, { status: 400 });\n   251→    }\n   252→\n   253→    values.push(listingId);\n   254→    db.prepare(`UPDATE listings SET ${updates.join(\", \")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...values);\n   255→\n   256→    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n   257→  } catch (error) {\n   258→    console.error(\"Update listing error:\", error);\n   259→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n   260→  }\n   261→}\n   262→\n   263→export async function DELETE(\n   264→  _req: NextRequest,\n   265→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   266→) {\n   267→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   268→  if (!session) {\n   269→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   270→  }\n   271→\n   272→  const { id } = await params;\n   273→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   274→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   275→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   276→  }\n   277→\n   278→  const listing = db\n   279→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   280→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   281→\n   282→  if (!listing) {\n   283→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n   284→  }\n   285→\n   286→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n   287→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can delete this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n   288→  }\n   289→\n   290→  // Cannot delete if a platform link has been shared\n   291→  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n   292→    return NextResponse.json(\n   293→      { error: \"Cannot delete a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   294→      { status: 400 }\n   295→    );\n   296→  }\n   297→\n   298→  try {\n   299→    // Get members to notify (excluding the author)\n   300→    const members = db\n   301→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != ?\")\n   302→      .all(listingId, session.userId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   303→\n   304→    // Delete in a transaction (notifications sent after, so they aren't wiped)\n   305→    const deleteTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n   306→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   307→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   308→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM notifications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   309→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM ratings WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   310→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   311→    });\n   312→\n   313→    deleteTransaction();\n   314→\n   315→    // Send deletion notifications after transaction (listing_id is NULL since listing is gone)\n   316→    for (const member of members) {\n   317→      try {\n   318→        createNotification(\n   319→          member.user_id,\n   320→          null,\n   321→          \"listing_deleted\",\n   322→          `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" has been cancelled by the organizer.`\n   323→        );\n   324→      } catch {\n   325→        // Notification failure should not block response\n   326→      }\n   327→    }\n   328→\n   329→    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n   330→  } catch (error) {\n   331→    console.error(\"Delete listing error:\", error);\n   332→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n   333→  }\n   334→}\n   335→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"bebdbd71-6626-4cec-8ab7-3174a187c5ee","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:01:15.539Z"}
