{"type":"system","subtype":"task_notification","task_id":"aa8122251812e89bc","tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","status":"completed","output_file":"","summary":"Code review round 8","usage":{"total_tokens":90024,"tool_uses":52,"duration_ms":367589},"uuid":"c06a44d0-a712-4a5b-b027-b4a06073a067","session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SoX6ZnKFwGoRApAVEkLejp","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have enough information to provide a thorough review. Let me compile my findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review: Bookmate Application\n\nReviewing all API routes, lib files, and client-side components. Here is what I found.\n\n---\n\n### Critical Issues\n\n**1. Author receives \"new member\" notification when a non-author joins — but the author IS always in `listing_members`, so the notification logic double-notifies on a max-2 group (Confidence: 90)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, line 99\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 117–144\n\nIn `notifyGroupFull`, non-author members are excluded using `user_id != author_id`. This is correct. But in the join flow, `notifyListingAuthor` is always called (line 99), even when the joiner fills the group and triggers `notifyGroupFull`. The author then receives two notifications: one \"Someone joined\" and one implicit \"group is full\" buried in the `notifyGroupFull` message that goes to non-author members only — so the author only gets the first. That's fine.\n\nThe actual bug is subtler: `notifyListingAuthor` at line 99 fires unconditionally for every join. When the author has a group of 2 and someone joins (filling it), the author gets a \"new member\" notification. That is correct behavior. However, `notifyGroupFull` at lines 117–144 sends the \"group is full\" message only to **non-author** members. The author never gets a \"group is full\" notification. This is an intentional choice per the comment, but the notification email subject `\"group_full\"` is defined in `getEmailSubject` (line 33) yet the author never receives it. This is a minor product gap, not a crash bug, so dropping it to borderline.\n\n**2. Race condition: `is_full` flag can be stale when the PATCH endpoint reopens a listing (Confidence: 85)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 226–238\n\nWhen `maxGroupSize` is updated, the code reads `currentMemberCount` outside a transaction and then conditionally adds `is_full = 0` to the UPDATE. This is a two-read-one-write pattern: the member count is read, then the UPDATE fires. Between those two operations, another concurrent join could complete (especially since `join/route.ts` uses its own transaction). If that happens, the listing gets reopened (`is_full = 0`) even though it was just filled by the concurrent join. This would allow additional joins to a group that should be closed.\n\nThe fix is to wrap the member count check and the UPDATE together in a transaction, or to set `is_full` based on the actual count at update time using a subquery:\n\n```sql\nUPDATE listings SET max_group_size = ?, is_full = CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?) < ? THEN 0 ELSE is_full END WHERE id = ?\n```\n\n**3. `pending_telegram_groups` table is never populated — the \"pending\" strategy for Telegram auto-linking is broken (Confidence: 85)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, lines 119–131\n\nThe webhook handler checks `pending_telegram_groups` (Strategy 1) for a mapping of `telegram_chat_id` to `listing_id`. However, no code path in the application ever inserts into `pending_telegram_groups`. The `auto-telegram` endpoint (`GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram`) only generates a deep link — it does not insert a row there. Strategy 1 is therefore dead code. Strategy 2 (the `/link` command fallback) works, but the comment in the webhook says \"check pending mapping table\" when that table is always empty. This means the fastest path (Strategy 1) silently fails for every bot-add event, and the bot sends the generic \"use /link command\" message every time. This is a functional regression — the auto-create flow described in the UI instructions never completes automatically.\n\n---\n\n### Important Issues\n\n**4. `notifyListingAuthor` is called even when the author joins their own listing — impossible but the guard is different in two places (Confidence: 80)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 29–32 and 99\n\nLine 30 returns a 400 if `listing.author_id === session.userId`, so `notifyListingAuthor` at line 99 can never be reached for the author. This is fine as-is. Not a bug, but worth confirming that the same guard exists in the approval flow.\n\nIn `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts` line 110: when an application is approved, `notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\")` fires for the approved user. But `notifyListingAuthor` is NOT called here — meaning the listing author does not get a \"new member\" notification when they approve someone via the approval flow. In the direct-join flow they do get it, but not when approvals are used. This is a missing notification for a real use case. Confidence: 82.\n\n**5. The `PATCH /api/listings/[id]` endpoint does not notify existing members when the listing is edited (Confidence: 82)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 248–256\n\nThe `listing_updated` notification type exists in `getEmailSubject` (notifications.ts line 47) and is referenced in the email subject handler, but `PATCH` never creates any notification. If a member joined a group with start date May 1 and the author edits it to April 1, the member is not notified. No emit of `listing_updated` anywhere in the codebase exists. This is a product gap that leaves members silently out of sync with listing changes.\n\n**6. The `GET /api/listings/[id]` leaks `telegram_chat_id` and `book_olid` to unauthenticated users (Confidence: 80)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 90–94\n\nThe code strips `telegram_link` and `discord_link` from the response for non-members. However `safeListingData` is a shallow spread of the full `listing` row from `SELECT l.*`, which also includes `telegram_chat_id` (an internal Telegram chat ID) and `book_olid`. The `telegram_chat_id` is the internal Telegram group ID — exposing this to anyone who can fetch the listing detail page (before `is_full` check) is an information leak. A motivated attacker who knows the chat ID can attempt to interact with the Telegram group directly. The fix is to explicitly allowlist fields in the listing response rather than using `SELECT l.*` + delete.\n\n---\n\n### Summary\n\n| # | Issue | File | Confidence |\n|---|-------|------|-----------|\n| 1 | Author never receives `group_full` notification | `lib/notifications.ts:117` | 80 |\n| 2 | Race condition reopening listing via PATCH max_group_size | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:226` | 85 |\n| 3 | `pending_telegram_groups` never populated — Strategy 1 Telegram auto-link is dead | `app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts:119` | 85 |\n| 4 | Author not notified when approving an application (approval flow missing `notifyListingAuthor`) | `app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts:110` | 82 |\n| 5 | No `listing_updated` notification sent to members on PATCH | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:254` | 82 |\n| 6 | `telegram_chat_id` leaked in listing GET response to non-members | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:90` | 80 |\n\n---\n\n### Detailed Fix Guidance\n\n**Issue 2 (race condition, PATCH max_group_size):** Wrap the size check and the full listing UPDATE in a single `db.transaction`. The current double `SELECT COUNT(*)` in this function (lines 186–188 and 232–234) is already a code smell.\n\n**Issue 3 (dead Strategy 1):** Either populate `pending_telegram_groups` when the deep link is generated in `GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram` (insert `(listingId, expectedChatId)` — though Telegram doesn't reveal the chat ID in advance, so the real fix is to embed the listing ID in the group name or use the `/start` payload, which is already Strategy 2/3). The simplest fix is to remove the dead Strategy 1 code path and document that only `/start` payload and `/link` command are supported.\n\n**Issue 4 (missing notification on approval):** In `applications/route.ts` after the `approveTransaction`, call `notifyListingAuthor(listingId, applicantName)`. The applicant's display name needs to be fetched from the DB at that point (it's not stored in the application row — add it to the `application` query or do a separate `SELECT display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?`).\n\n**Issue 6 (field leak):** Replace `SELECT l.*` in the listing GET with an explicit column list that omits `telegram_chat_id`, or add `delete safeListingData.telegram_chat_id` alongside the existing deletions at lines 92–93."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: aa8122251812e89bc (use SendMessage with to: 'aa8122251812e89bc' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 90599\ntool_uses: 52\nduration_ms: 367591</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"6c038d7b-d5fc-4f62-a022-9e90f2afcbcc","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:07:10.061Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Do a thorough code review of the Bookmate application at /root/Projects/bookmate/app. This is a Next.js 15 App Router application with SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session auth, and TypeScript.\n\nFocus on finding HIGH-CONFIDENCE bugs, security issues, logic errors, or code quality problems. Previous reviews (rounds 1-7) have already fixed:\n- HTML injection in Telegram messages\n- Telegram /start auth bypass\n- Discord snowflake validation\n- Email normalization\n- Rate limiting\n- Input validation\n- Session security\n- Transaction safety\n- Logout bugs\n- Various notification bugs\n\nLook for issues that may have been MISSED in previous reviews. Focus on:\n1. API route handlers - any unhandled edge cases, missing auth checks, or data leaks\n2. Database queries - SQL injection risks, missing indexes, race conditions\n3. Client-side code - XSS, CSRF, state management bugs\n4. Error handling - unhandled exceptions that could crash the server\n5. Business logic - group formation, notifications, listing lifecycle edge cases\n6. Any new patterns that could be problematic\n\nBe very selective - only report issues you're confident are real bugs or significant problems. Provide file paths and line numbers.","agentId":"aa8122251812e89bc","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have enough information to provide a thorough review. Let me compile my findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review: Bookmate Application\n\nReviewing all API routes, lib files, and client-side components. Here is what I found.\n\n---\n\n### Critical Issues\n\n**1. Author receives \"new member\" notification when a non-author joins — but the author IS always in `listing_members`, so the notification logic double-notifies on a max-2 group (Confidence: 90)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, line 99\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 117–144\n\nIn `notifyGroupFull`, non-author members are excluded using `user_id != author_id`. This is correct. But in the join flow, `notifyListingAuthor` is always called (line 99), even when the joiner fills the group and triggers `notifyGroupFull`. The author then receives two notifications: one \"Someone joined\" and one implicit \"group is full\" buried in the `notifyGroupFull` message that goes to non-author members only — so the author only gets the first. That's fine.\n\nThe actual bug is subtler: `notifyListingAuthor` at line 99 fires unconditionally for every join. When the author has a group of 2 and someone joins (filling it), the author gets a \"new member\" notification. That is correct behavior. However, `notifyGroupFull` at lines 117–144 sends the \"group is full\" message only to **non-author** members. The author never gets a \"group is full\" notification. This is an intentional choice per the comment, but the notification email subject `\"group_full\"` is defined in `getEmailSubject` (line 33) yet the author never receives it. This is a minor product gap, not a crash bug, so dropping it to borderline.\n\n**2. Race condition: `is_full` flag can be stale when the PATCH endpoint reopens a listing (Confidence: 85)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 226–238\n\nWhen `maxGroupSize` is updated, the code reads `currentMemberCount` outside a transaction and then conditionally adds `is_full = 0` to the UPDATE. This is a two-read-one-write pattern: the member count is read, then the UPDATE fires. Between those two operations, another concurrent join could complete (especially since `join/route.ts` uses its own transaction). If that happens, the listing gets reopened (`is_full = 0`) even though it was just filled by the concurrent join. This would allow additional joins to a group that should be closed.\n\nThe fix is to wrap the member count check and the UPDATE together in a transaction, or to set `is_full` based on the actual count at update time using a subquery:\n\n```sql\nUPDATE listings SET max_group_size = ?, is_full = CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?) < ? THEN 0 ELSE is_full END WHERE id = ?\n```\n\n**3. `pending_telegram_groups` table is never populated — the \"pending\" strategy for Telegram auto-linking is broken (Confidence: 85)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, lines 119–131\n\nThe webhook handler checks `pending_telegram_groups` (Strategy 1) for a mapping of `telegram_chat_id` to `listing_id`. However, no code path in the application ever inserts into `pending_telegram_groups`. The `auto-telegram` endpoint (`GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram`) only generates a deep link — it does not insert a row there. Strategy 1 is therefore dead code. Strategy 2 (the `/link` command fallback) works, but the comment in the webhook says \"check pending mapping table\" when that table is always empty. This means the fastest path (Strategy 1) silently fails for every bot-add event, and the bot sends the generic \"use /link command\" message every time. This is a functional regression — the auto-create flow described in the UI instructions never completes automatically.\n\n---\n\n### Important Issues\n\n**4. `notifyListingAuthor` is called even when the author joins their own listing — impossible but the guard is different in two places (Confidence: 80)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 29–32 and 99\n\nLine 30 returns a 400 if `listing.author_id === session.userId`, so `notifyListingAuthor` at line 99 can never be reached for the author. This is fine as-is. Not a bug, but worth confirming that the same guard exists in the approval flow.\n\nIn `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts` line 110: when an application is approved, `notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\")` fires for the approved user. But `notifyListingAuthor` is NOT called here — meaning the listing author does not get a \"new member\" notification when they approve someone via the approval flow. In the direct-join flow they do get it, but not when approvals are used. This is a missing notification for a real use case. Confidence: 82.\n\n**5. The `PATCH /api/listings/[id]` endpoint does not notify existing members when the listing is edited (Confidence: 82)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 248–256\n\nThe `listing_updated` notification type exists in `getEmailSubject` (notifications.ts line 47) and is referenced in the email subject handler, but `PATCH` never creates any notification. If a member joined a group with start date May 1 and the author edits it to April 1, the member is not notified. No emit of `listing_updated` anywhere in the codebase exists. This is a product gap that leaves members silently out of sync with listing changes.\n\n**6. The `GET /api/listings/[id]` leaks `telegram_chat_id` and `book_olid` to unauthenticated users (Confidence: 80)**\n\nFile: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 90–94\n\nThe code strips `telegram_link` and `discord_link` from the response for non-members. However `safeListingData` is a shallow spread of the full `listing` row from `SELECT l.*`, which also includes `telegram_chat_id` (an internal Telegram chat ID) and `book_olid`. The `telegram_chat_id` is the internal Telegram group ID — exposing this to anyone who can fetch the listing detail page (before `is_full` check) is an information leak. A motivated attacker who knows the chat ID can attempt to interact with the Telegram group directly. The fix is to explicitly allowlist fields in the listing response rather than using `SELECT l.*` + delete.\n\n---\n\n### Summary\n\n| # | Issue | File | Confidence |\n|---|-------|------|-----------|\n| 1 | Author never receives `group_full` notification | `lib/notifications.ts:117` | 80 |\n| 2 | Race condition reopening listing via PATCH max_group_size | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:226` | 85 |\n| 3 | `pending_telegram_groups` never populated — Strategy 1 Telegram auto-link is dead | `app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts:119` | 85 |\n| 4 | Author not notified when approving an application (approval flow missing `notifyListingAuthor`) | `app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts:110` | 82 |\n| 5 | No `listing_updated` notification sent to members on PATCH | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:254` | 82 |\n| 6 | `telegram_chat_id` leaked in listing GET response to non-members | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:90` | 80 |\n\n---\n\n### Detailed Fix Guidance\n\n**Issue 2 (race condition, PATCH max_group_size):** Wrap the size check and the full listing UPDATE in a single `db.transaction`. The current double `SELECT COUNT(*)` in this function (lines 186–188 and 232–234) is already a code smell.\n\n**Issue 3 (dead Strategy 1):** Either populate `pending_telegram_groups` when the deep link is generated in `GET /api/listings/[id]/auto-telegram` (insert `(listingId, expectedChatId)` — though Telegram doesn't reveal the chat ID in advance, so the real fix is to embed the listing ID in the group name or use the `/start` payload, which is already Strategy 2/3). The simplest fix is to remove the dead Strategy 1 code path and document that only `/start` payload and `/link` command are supported.\n\n**Issue 4 (missing notification on approval):** In `applications/route.ts` after the `approveTransaction`, call `notifyListingAuthor(listingId, applicantName)`. The applicant's display name needs to be fetched from the DB at that point (it's not stored in the application row — add it to the `application` query or do a separate `SELECT display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?`).\n\n**Issue 6 (field leak):** Replace `SELECT l.*` in the listing GET with an explicit column list that omits `telegram_chat_id`, or add `delete safeListingData.telegram_chat_id` alongside the existing deletions at lines 92–93."}],"totalDurationMs":367591,"totalTokens":90599,"totalToolUseCount":52,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":464,"cache_read_input_tokens":87790,"output_tokens":2344,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":464},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NELs1rukyDDwFFf7RJA2YE","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function PATCH(\n     7→  req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const body = await req.json();\n    34→  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n    35→\n    36→  if (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n    44→  const application = db\n    45→    .prepare(\n    46→      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    47→    )\n    48→    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    49→\n    50→  if (!application) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n    53→      { status: 404 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n    58→\n    59→  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    60→    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    61→    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    62→      // Check if group is already full\n    63→      const { count } = db\n    64→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    65→        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    66→\n    67→      if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    68→        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      // Update application status\n    72→      db.prepare(\n    73→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    74→      ).run(applicationId);\n    75→\n    76→      // Add to members\n    77→      db.prepare(\n    78→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    79→      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n    80→\n    81→      // Check if group is now full\n    82→      const newCount = count + 1;\n    83→      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n    84→      if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    85→        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    86→\n    87→        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n    88→        rejectedApps = db\n    89→          .prepare(\n    90→            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    91→          )\n    92→          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n    93→\n    94→        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n    95→          db.prepare(\n    96→            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    97→          ).run(app.id);\n    98→        }\n    99→\n   100→        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n   101→      }\n   102→      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n   103→    });\n   104→\n   105→    try {\n   106→      const result = approveTransaction();\n   107→\n   108→      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n   109→      try {\n   110→        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n   111→\n   112→        if (result.isFull) {\n   113→          notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n   114→          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n   115→            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   116→          }\n   117→        }\n   118→      } catch (notifErr) {\n   119→        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n   120→      }\n   121→\n   122→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   123→    } catch (err) {\n   124→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n   125→      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n   126→    }\n   127→  } else {\n   128→    // Reject\n   129→    db.prepare(\n   130→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   131→    ).run(applicationId);\n   132→\n   133→    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   134→\n   135→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   136→  }\n   137→}\n   138→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"8d1be86e-2cfc-46b4-9dd0-a27cff4941bf","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:07:25.511Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\nexport async function PATCH(\n  req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n  }\n\n  const body = await req.json();\n  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n\n  if (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n  const application = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    )\n    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!application) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n      { status: 404 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n\n  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n      // Check if group is already full\n      const { count } = db\n        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n\n      if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n      }\n\n      // Update application status\n      db.prepare(\n        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n      ).run(applicationId);\n\n      // Add to members\n      db.prepare(\n        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n\n      // Check if group is now full\n      const newCount = count + 1;\n      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n      if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n\n        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n        rejectedApps = db\n          .prepare(\n            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n          )\n          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n\n        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n          db.prepare(\n            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n          ).run(app.id);\n        }\n\n        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n      }\n      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n    });\n\n    try {\n      const result = approveTransaction();\n\n      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n      try {\n        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n\n        if (result.isFull) {\n          notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n          }\n        }\n      } catch (notifErr) {\n        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n      }\n\n      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    } catch (err) {\n      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n  } else {\n    // Reject\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    ).run(applicationId);\n\n    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n\n    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n  }\n}\n","numLines":138,"startLine":1,"totalLines":138}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019KFP9fGVyCj4EgNDjRePYb","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→\n     4→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     5→  const user = db\n     6→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     7→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     8→  return user?.email || null;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→export function createNotification(\n    12→  userId: number,\n    13→  listingId: number | null,\n    14→  type: string,\n    15→  message: string\n    16→) {\n    17→  db.prepare(\n    18→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    19→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    20→\n    21→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    22→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    23→  if (email) {\n    24→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    25→    sendEmail(email, subject, message).catch(() => {\n    26→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    27→    });\n    28→  }\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    32→  switch (type) {\n    33→    case \"new_member\":\n    34→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    35→    case \"group_full\":\n    36→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    37→    case \"application_received\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    39→    case \"application_approved\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    41→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    43→    case \"rating_received\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    45→    case \"listing_deleted\":\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been cancelled\";\n    47→    case \"listing_updated\":\n    48→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been updated\";\n    49→    case \"telegram_ready\":\n    50→      return \"Bookmate: Your Telegram reading group is ready!\";\n    51→    case \"discord_ready\":\n    52→      return \"Bookmate: Your Discord channel is ready!\";\n    53→    default:\n    54→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    55→  }\n    56→}\n    57→\n    58→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    59→  listingId: number,\n    60→  joinerName: string\n    61→) {\n    62→  const listing = db\n    63→    .prepare(\n    64→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    65→    )\n    66→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    67→\n    68→  if (listing) {\n    69→    createNotification(\n    70→      listing.author_id,\n    71→      listingId,\n    72→      \"new_member\",\n    73→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    74→    );\n    75→  }\n    76→}\n    77→\n    78→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    79→  listingId: number,\n    80→  applicantName: string\n    81→) {\n    82→  const listing = db\n    83→    .prepare(\n    84→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    85→    )\n    86→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    87→\n    88→  if (listing) {\n    89→    createNotification(\n    90→      listing.author_id,\n    91→      listingId,\n    92→      \"application_received\",\n    93→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    94→    );\n    95→  }\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n    99→  userId: number,\n   100→  listingId: number,\n   101→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n   102→) {\n   103→  const listing = db\n   104→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   105→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   106→\n   107→  if (listing) {\n   108→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   109→    const message =\n   110→      decision === \"approved\"\n   111→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   112→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   113→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   114→  }\n   115→}\n   116→\n   117→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   118→  const listing = db\n   119→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   120→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string; author_id: number } | undefined;\n   121→  // Exclude the author — they already received a separate \"new_member\" notification\n   122→  const members = db\n   123→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != ?\")\n   124→    .all(listingId, listing?.author_id ?? -1) as { user_id: number }[];\n   125→\n   126→  if (listing) {\n   127→    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n   128→    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n   129→      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n   130→      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   131→    const message = botConfigured\n   132→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n   133→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n   134→\n   135→    for (const member of members) {\n   136→      createNotification(\n   137→        member.user_id,\n   138→        listingId,\n   139→        \"group_full\",\n   140→        message\n   141→      );\n   142→    }\n   143→  }\n   144→}\n   145→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","uuid":"a2068fd7-a079-4ed7-b978-4a5c9eb41292","timestamp":"2026-03-24T01:07:25.536Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts","content":"import db from \"./db\";\nimport { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n\nfunction getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n  const user = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n  return user?.email || null;\n}\n\nexport function createNotification(\n  userId: number,\n  listingId: number | null,\n  type: string,\n  message: string\n) {\n  db.prepare(\n    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n\n  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n  if (email) {\n    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    sendEmail(email, subject, message).catch(() => {\n      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    });\n  }\n}\n\nfunction getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n  switch (type) {\n    case \"new_member\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    case \"group_full\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    case \"application_received\":\n      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    case \"application_approved\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    case \"application_rejected\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    case \"rating_received\":\n      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    case \"listing_deleted\":\n      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been cancelled\";\n    case \"listing_updated\":\n      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been updated\";\n    case \"telegram_ready\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your Telegram reading group is ready!\";\n    case \"discord_ready\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your Discord channel is ready!\";\n    default:\n      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyListingAuthor(\n  listingId: number,\n  joinerName: string\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    createNotification(\n      listing.author_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"new_member\",\n      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyApplicationReceived(\n  listingId: number,\n  applicantName: string\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    createNotification(\n      listing.author_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"application_received\",\n      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyApplicationDecision(\n  userId: number,\n  listingId: number,\n  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n    const message =\n      decision === \"approved\"\n        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string; author_id: number } | undefined;\n  // Exclude the author — they already received a separate \"new_member\" notification\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != ?\")\n    .all(listingId, listing?.author_id ?? -1) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n    const message = botConfigured\n      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n\n    for (const member of members) {\n      createNotification(\n        member.user_id,\n        listingId,\n        \"group_full\",\n        message\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n","numLines":145,"startLine":1,"totalLines":145}}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"message_stop"},"session_id":"4c5466fa-2333-4a25-bba1-4cb6c49f2287","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"c63708e6-82b2-44e6-83b1-bbd2060c2edd"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01W7zwD9nxgCmGzrF4MXznm8","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession, requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→import { isBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     6→import { isBotConfigured as isDiscordBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     7→\n     8→export async function GET(\n     9→  _req: NextRequest,\n    10→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    11→) {\n    12→  const { id } = await params;\n    13→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    14→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\n    20→      `SELECT\n    21→        l.*,\n    22→        u.display_name as author_name\n    23→      FROM listings l\n    24→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    25→      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    26→    )\n    27→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    28→\n    29→  if (!listing) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const members = db\n    34→    .prepare(\n    35→      `SELECT u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, lm.joined_at\n    36→       FROM listing_members lm\n    37→       JOIN users u ON lm.user_id = u.id\n    38→       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n    39→       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    40→    )\n    41→    .all(listingId) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n    42→\n    43→  const session = await getSession();\n    44→  const isMember = session.userId\n    45→    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    46→    : false;\n    47→  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    48→    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    49→    : false;\n    50→\n    51→  // PRD: full listings should be invisible to non-participants\n    52→  if (listing.is_full && !isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json(\n    54→      { error: \"This listing is no longer available\" },\n    55→      { status: 404 }\n    56→    );\n    57→  }\n    58→\n    59→  // Check if the current user has an application (regardless of requires_approval,\n    60→  // since the flag may have been toggled after the application was submitted)\n    61→  let hasApplied = false;\n    62→  let applicationStatus = \"\";\n    63→  if (session.userId && !isMember) {\n    64→    const app = db\n    65→      .prepare(\n    66→        \"SELECT status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    67→      )\n    68→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { status: string } | undefined;\n    69→    if (app) {\n    70→      hasApplied = app.status === \"pending\";\n    71→      applicationStatus = app.status;\n    72→    }\n    73→  }\n    74→\n    75→  // Fetch pending applicants if the viewer is the author\n    76→  let pendingApplicants: { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[] = [];\n    77→  if (isAuthor && listing.requires_approval) {\n    78→    pendingApplicants = db\n    79→      .prepare(\n    80→        `SELECT la.id as application_id, u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, la.applied_at\n    81→         FROM listing_applications la\n    82→         JOIN users u ON la.user_id = u.id\n    83→         WHERE la.listing_id = ? AND la.status = 'pending'\n    84→         ORDER BY la.applied_at ASC`\n    85→      )\n    86→      .all(listingId) as { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[];\n    87→  }\n    88→\n    89→  // Strip platform links for non-members to prevent leaking invite URLs\n    90→  const safeListingData = { ...listing };\n    91→  if (!isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    92→    delete safeListingData.telegram_link;\n    93→    delete safeListingData.discord_link;\n    94→  }\n    95→\n    96→  return NextResponse.json({\n    97→    listing: {\n    98→      ...safeListingData,\n    99→      members,\n   100→      memberCount: members.length,\n   101→      isMember,\n   102→      isAuthor,\n   103→      hasApplied,\n   104→      applicationStatus,\n   105→      pendingApplicants,\n   106→      currentUserId: session.userId || null,\n   107→      telegramBotConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n   108→      discordBotConfigured: isDiscordBotConfigured(),\n   109→    },\n   110→  });\n   111→}\n   112→\n   113→export async function PATCH(\n   114→  req: NextRequest,\n   115→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   116→) {\n   117→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   118→  if (!session) {\n   119→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   120→  }\n   121→\n   122→  const { id } = await params;\n   123→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   124→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   125→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   126→  }\n   127→\n   128→  const listing = db\n   129→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   130→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   131→\n
